name: Everett Lee
abstract: Everett Astor Lee (August 31, 1916 – January 12, 2022) was an American symphonic conductor, opera music director, violinist and music scholar. He was the first African American to conduct a Broadway musical, the first to "conduct an established symphony orchestra below the Mason–Dixon line", and the first to conduct a performance by a ma…

concerts.label: Tribute to Sylvia Olden Lee, Master Musician and Teacher
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: I got some of my -- I was inspired and it was more gratifying for some of the things I did with Everett Lee and Dean Dixon... I played concerts with both of them.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: when Everett Lee came back to the States to do a tour, he chose to do my piece [Kosbro] with the New York Philharmonic.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Elgar
about_quotes.evidence: There's a young fella I met about four or five years ago. He's originally from Cleveland. He's living in New York now. Violinist by the name of Everett Lee.
